data.title: Velo3D Announces Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Financial Results; Unveils Long-Term Capacity Plan Envisioning up to Approximately 400 Production Systems
data.description: In 2025, the company reported a revenue of $46 million with a backlog of $31 million as of December 31st. For 2026, they anticipate revenue to fall between $60 million and $70 million. The company aims to achieve positive EBITDA in the latter half of 2026. Additionally, they revealed a demand-based capacity strategy that could see an increase in production to around 400 units.

data.title: Maven Trading Launches WenCrypto, a Dedicated Crypto-Only Prop Firm Built by Proven Operators
data.description: A new firm called WenCrypto has been launched by Maven Trading in Dover, Del., on March 24, 2026. WenCrypto is a proprietary trading company exclusively for crypto assets that aims to provide digital asset traders with a trading platform tailored to their needs. This initiative addresses the trading methods commonly utilized by crypto traders and seeks to enhance their trading experience.

data.title: Google's Quantum AI integrates neutral atom computing into their superconducting roadmap, led by Adam Kaufman in Boulder.
data.description: Expanding its quantum computing efforts, Google has included neutral atom qubits to its strategy and brought JILA's Adam Kaufman on board to lead the hardware team based in Boulder. This team will work alongside the existing superconducting program. This move signifies a significant step forward in Google's quantum computing mission, as it seeks to harness the potential of neutral atom qubits.

data.title: Governor Signs Emergency Legislation Restoring HIV Medication Access for More Than 12,000 Floridians
data.description: Governor Ron DeSantis of Florida has approved emergency legislation aimed at reinstating access to vital HIV medications for over 12,000 residents who were left without coverage following cuts to the state's AIDS Drug Assistance Program by the Florida Department of Health on March 1. The bill, passed unanimously by both houses of the Florida Legislature, allocates $30.9 million in emergency bridge funding until June 30, 2026. This move effectively restores the state's AIDS Drug Assistance Program, providing crucial support for those affected by HIV in Florida.

data.title: Philadelphia International Airport and MarketPlace PHL Set Guinness World Record for ‘Longest Line of Cheesesteaks’ on National Cheesesteak Day
data.description: In Philadelphia, passengers at Philadelphia International Airport (PHL) witnessed culinary history as the "Longest Line of Cheesesteaks" set a new Guinness World Record for National Cheesesteak Day, March 24. The line of sandwiches at PHL reached an impressive 1,291 cheesesteaks, equivalent to more than three football fields in length, occupying almost the entire concourse between terminal.

data.title: NEWS MEDIA ADVISORY: Ray Kurzweil Keynotes Absolute Security Resilient CISO Summit During RSAC 2026
data.description: Seattle and San Francisco—Absolute Security, a top enterprise cyber resilience provider, shared that futurist and AI expert Ray Kurzweil will be the keynote speaker at the upcoming Resilient CISO Summit. This event, organized by RSAC, will showcase Kurzweil's thoughts on "Cybersecurity in the Age of Exponential Intelligence." The summit will delve into the future of AI and its significance for cybersecurity leadership. The Absolute Security President & CEO will also participate in the program.

data.description: Despite the president's assurance of quick aid, Americans may experience lasting financial repercussions from the conflict long after its conclusion.

data.title: Are BTBD, RMBI, DBRG, WBD Obtaining Fair Deals for their Shareholders?
data.description: Potential gains for insiders surpass what regular shareholders can receive. The suggested deals might have conditions restricting better rival bids. Shareholders should reach out to the company to explore their entitlements and choices for free.
